description: Load the complete 24-subunit biological assembly of human H-chain ferritin (PDB: 1FHA) into PyMOL. Do not just load the asymmetric unit monomer; you must fetch or construct the full 24-mer biological assembly. Color the subunits distinctly to highlight the architecture. Measure the inner diameter of the hollow core cavity (across opposite subunits) and the outer diameter of the complete protein cage. Identify the two highly conserved acidic residues (Asp and Glu) that line the narrowest part of the 3-fold symmetry channels (the iron entry pores). Save a publication-quality figure of the 24-mer cage to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ferritin_cage.png. Write a report to ~/PyMOL_Data/ferritin_analysis_report.txt that includes: 1) Confirmation it is a 24-mer, 2) The measured inner diameter in Angstroms, 3) The measured outer diameter in Angstroms, 4) The names and residue numbers of the two pore-lining acidic residues.
